reasoning: Elon Musk has securely held the CEO position at Tesla since 2008, deeply intertwining his personal brand, leadership, and long-term vision (specifically regarding AI and robotics) with the company's valuation. While he has historically complained about the administrative burdens of being a CEO, the mid-2024 shareholder vote re-approving his massive 2018 pay package strongly reaffirmed shareholder support and provides an immense financial incentive for him to remain in his current capacity. While he eventually passed the CEO title to Linda Yaccarino at X (formerly Twitter), transitioning at Tesla would be vastly more sensitive given its status as a publicly traded trillion-dollar-level company, and no obvious, well-prepped successor has been formally positioned. Barring significant and sudden health issues, extreme regulatory intervention from the SEC, or a rapid decision to transition his title strictly to "Product Architect" or "CTO", he is highly likely to remain CEO through the end of 2025. Accounting for the baseline statistical chances of departure across 15+ months (including mortality and scandal tail risks), an 8% probability reflects the most appropriate expectation.
